description: A designed ICE 'NEWS RELEASE' announcement graphic, square format. The background is a solid deep navy-blue field with a subtle woodgrain texture. Centered upper area shows the gray DHS/ICE seal beside the agency name 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' in gray serif type, underlined by a thin red horizontal rule. Below, the words 'NEWS RELEASE' appear in very large bold white sans-serif capitals, followed by a white serif subhead reading 'Detainer placed on person of interest in connection with California fires.' There is no photograph; this is a clean, text-only branded template the agency uses to push out press statements on social media. The accompanying tweet says ICE ERO placed a detainer on Juan Manuel Sierra, a person of interest connected to the California wildfires. The messaging links an immigrant to a high-profile disaster, a technique that ties the deportation agenda to public-safety fears. Target audience: news media and general public.
notable_text: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement; NEWS RELEASE; Detainer placed on person of interest in connection with California fires
